data.content:
Implementation plan for canonical Knots lease identity

1. Canonical identity source
- Tighten `src/lib/agent-identity.ts` so execution/lease metadata comes only from canonical fields: `agent_type`, `provider`, explicit `agent_name` when present, `lease_model`, and `version`.
- Stop deriving execution metadata from display-only fields such as `label`, and separate display-label formatting from canonical lease metadata so UI labels can remain friendly without leaking into Knots identity.
- Confirm whether fallback behavior should omit `agent_name` entirely when it is not explicitly configured, or keep only a minimal provider/command fallback that does not depend on `label`.

2. Knots command plumbing
- Extend the Knots wrapper option types in `src/lib/knots-operations.ts` and the shared execution metadata shape in `src/lib/execution-port.ts` only as needed to represent canonical fields explicitly.
- Keep `src/lib/knots-lease-runtime.ts` as the source of truth for lease creation, sending canonical metadata to `kno lease create` via `agent_type`, `provider`, `agent_name`, `model` (from `lease_model`), and `model_version`.
- Update `src/lib/execution-backend-helpers.ts`, `src/lib/execution-backend.ts`, `src/lib/terminal-manager.ts`, and `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-agent.ts` so claim/poll/rotation paths reuse the same canonical `ExecutionAgentInfo` object instead of rebuilding identity from `label`, `agent.model`, or ad hoc normalization at the call site.
- Because current `kno claim` and `kno poll` only accept `agent-name`, `agent-model`, and `agent-version`, rely on the bound lease metadata for `provider` and `agent_type`, and ensure the values passed for claim/poll are the canonical `agent_name`, `lease_model`, and `version` fields.

3. Verification strategy
- Update `src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity.test.ts` to assert canonical execution metadata no longer uses configured display labels as `agentName`, while display helpers still render the label correctly.
- Extend `src/lib/__tests__/knots-coverage-crud.test.ts` and `src/lib/__tests__/knots-integration-operations.test.ts` so lease creation, claim, and poll calls assert the exact canonical arguments passed through the wrapper layer.
- Extend `src/lib/__tests__/execution-backend-rollback.test.ts`, `src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-knots-lease.test.ts`, and `src/lib/__tests__/local-worker.test.ts` to verify terminal-manager and structured execution flows create leases and prepare claim/poll work with canonical metadata end to end.
- Add representative Claude, Codex, and OpenCode fixtures/mocks that prove `kno lease list --json`, `kno claim --json`, and `kno show --json` expose matching canonical lease metadata once the wrapper paths are wired consistently.

4. Complexity and risks
- Complexity: medium. The code changes are localized, but the same metadata is threaded through multiple entry points and test doubles.
- Primary risk: existing tests intentionally treat `label` as execution metadata, so refactoring must preserve display behavior while changing only Knots-facing identity.
- Secondary risk: `kno claim`/`kno poll` CLI flags currently cover only name/model/version, so Foolery must avoid inventing parallel identity sources and instead depend on lease stamping for provider/type.

Hierarchy
- No child knots are needed for planning. This work is still a single implementation knot with one coordinated change set.
